labels.105: The feature activates on noun phrases or descriptive content that characterizes or lists types of things or people.
labels.397: Phrases describing inherent qualities or characteristics that define someone's nature or worth.
labels.599: Adjectives or descriptive phrases emphasizing positive qualities, specialness, or excellence following conjunctions or transitional words.
labels.1232: Adjectives or descriptive phrases placed between "a/an" and noun phrases.
labels.1642: Phrases describing future or upcoming events, options, or time periods that are about to happen.
labels.1782: Sequences of product categories or navigation menu items separated by pipe delimiters.
labels.2933: Punctuation or special characters marking transitions between metadata sections or structured data fields.
labels.4001: Pipe characters separating structured data fields or form elements in semi-formatted text.
labels.4239: Phrases describing benefits or features positioned between comparative/contrastive language structures.
labels.4292: Phrases listing multiple related items or concepts separated by commas before a conjunction.
labels.4966: Phrases expressing intention, desire, or capability to perform an action or make something happen.
labels.5018: Feature activates on text fragments containing proper nouns, titles, or descriptive phrases positioned between clauses or appositives.
labels.6087: The feature fires on text passages containing proper nouns (names of people) surrounded by context words on both sides.
labels.6333: URL path or domain segment separators and identifiers in web addresses.
labels.6678: Text describing consequences or results that follow from a preceding action or condition.
labels.6889: Professional titles and credentials within biographical or organizational context.
labels.7089: Names or identifiers appearing between delimiters or punctuation marks in structured text fields.
labels.7093: Names or name fragments enclosed in parentheses or preceded by punctuation marks.
labels.7309: Pipe or bar character separating distinct sections or navigation elements in structured web content.
labels.7416: The feature activates on alphanumeric strings that appear to be identifiers or codes (file hashes, video IDs, numerical sequences).
labels.7720: Personal names or titles of people being introduced or credited in descriptive contexts.
labels.8926: Future tense or intention expressions introducing a contrasting or additional action.
labels.9070: Transitions between structured metadata fields or navigation elements separated by pipes and special characters.
labels.9491: Pipe characters or structural delimiters separating metadata fields and labels from their corresponding values.
labels.9788: The feature activates on phrases describing or defining what something is or has become, often with "to be" or state-of-being verbs between the markers.
labels.9902: Phrases describing fear, darkness, death, or unsettling imagery appearing in descriptive contexts.
labels.10547: Geographic location descriptors following "in" or "of" that specify places or regions.
labels.11071: Personal titles or names of people being introduced or referenced in context.
labels.11310: The feature activates on phrases describing negative or contrary conditions, particularly "not/no longer/might not be possible" expressing absence or impossibility.
labels.11495: Transitions between domain names or URLs and file/path extensions or subsequent content.
labels.12072: The feature activates on phrases introducing or explaining consequences, results, or implications of preceding statements.
labels.12076: Text describing methods, processes, or mechanisms that explain or clarify how something works or achieves a result.
labels.12874: Colons or punctuation marks separating titles or headings from descriptive text or subtitles.
labels.13133: Alphanumeric codes and identifiers extracted from structured metadata or URLs.
labels.13772: Numerical values or decimal numbers appearing between delimiters or in structured data contexts.
labels.14039: Pipe or double-pipe delimiters separating structured data fields or navigation elements.
labels.14060: Phrases indicating contrast or exception between clauses (e.g., "even if", "Besides", "instead of").
labels.14249: Alphanumeric strings or identifiers containing hyphens or dashes separating numeric or character sequences.
labels.15045: Contrast between a quality or characteristic and its practical consequence or application.
labels.15166: Transitions between contrasting clauses or ideas marked by discourse connectives like "And so," "So," "But," or "Remember" followed by shifts in subject or perspective.
labels.15232: Numeric sequences and alphanumeric codes containing "640799" or similar digit patterns mixed with letters like "US" and "B1".
labels.15775: Tokens separating or connecting structured metadata fields like names, dates, and file information.
labels.15879: The feature activates on alphanumeric strings and identifiers within URLs and file names.
labels.15991: Geographic location descriptions introducing specific place names or regions.
labels.16516: Phrases describing future plans or possibilities using "to" or future-oriented language with modal verbs.
labels.17417: The feature detects pipe characters (|) or structural delimiters that separate table headers or list columns.
labels.17616: Phrases that introduce or conclude clauses, often marking transitions between main ideas or quoted content.
labels.17765: The feature activates on contrasts or transitions between opposing concepts separated by "and" or similar connectors.
labels.17813: Transition between describing product features and their practical benefits or usage outcomes.
labels.18010: Person names or titles appearing after dates or in formal biographical/professional contexts.
